# CommitMe user-visible UI output

Generated by `test/user-visible-ui.test.mjs`. This fixture captures the command text, dialogs, notifications, and safety copy a user sees when running CommitMe. Commit hashes and temporary paths are normalized.

## Command feature matrix

| User input | Mode | Confirmation | Steering prompt |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `/commitme` | commit | no | — |
| `/commitme --confirm` | commit | yes | — |
| `/commitme --confirm focus on parser support` | commit | yes | focus on parser support |
| `/commitme --steering focus on parser support` | commit | no | focus on parser support |
| `/commitme --steering=focus on parser support` | commit | no | focus on parser support |
| `/commitme -- --prefer feat if accurate` | commit | no | --prefer feat if accurate |
| `/commitme help` | help | no | — |

## Help panel

Message card: `customType=commitme`, `display=true`.

```md
# CommitMe help

CommitMe creates a one-line Lightweight Conventional Commit subject from your current git changes.

## Commands

### /commitme [steering prompt]
Generates a one-line commit subject, stages the gathered changed paths, and creates a local git commit.
Optional steering text guides the draft when it matches the actual git changes.

### /commitme --confirm [steering prompt]
Generates a one-line commit subject, shows a confirmation prompt with that subject, and commits only if you confirm.

### /commitme --steering <prompt>
Passes explicit steering text (`--steering=<prompt>` also works). This is equivalent to positional steering text and works with `--confirm`.

### /commitme -- --prompt-that-starts-with-a-dash
Use `--` before positional steering text that begins with `-` or `--`.

### /commitme help
Shows this help panel. `/commitme --help` and `/commitme -h` work too.

## Commit message standard

Format: `<type>(optional-scope): <summary>`

Allowed types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `docs`, `test`, `chore`, `build`, `ci`, `perf`, `style`, `revert`.

Rules:
- Use imperative mood: `add`, `fix`, `remove`; not `added` or `fixed`.
- Keep the summary clear and specific.
- Do not end the summary with a period.
- Use a scope when it helps identify the affected area.
- CommitMe creates subject-only commits: no body, footer, bullets, or explanations.

## Safety

- CommitMe never runs `git push`.
- Secret-like, generated, and binary file contents are omitted from model context.
```

## Successful commit message

```text
Committed <commit-hash>: feat(commitme): add user-visible UI fixture
```

## Unknown flag warning

```text
WARNING: CommitMe: Unknown flag: --push
```

## Waiting and no-change notifications

```text
INFO: CommitMe: waiting for the current agent turn to finish...
WARNING: CommitMe: no staged or unstaged git changes found.
```

## Commit confirmation dialog

```text
Title: CommitMe: create commit?

Body:
Commit with this message?

feat(commitme): add user-visible UI fixture
```

Cancellation notification:

```text
INFO: CommitMe: commit cancelled.
```

## Unsafe-file approval dialog

```text
Title: CommitMe: commit potentially unsafe files?

Body:
CommitMe found changed files that look unsafe to stage:

- .env (known secret path)

Only continue if you reviewed these files and they contain safe fixtures/placeholders, not real secrets.
Block the commit if you are unsure.

Commit these flagged files?
```

Blocked notification:

```text
WARNING: CommitMe: commit blocked because potentially unsafe files were not approved.
```

## Representative unsafe-file approval copy for privacy-pattern fixtures

```text
CommitMe found changed files that look unsafe to stage:

- src/privacy/secret-patterns.ts (high-confidence secret-like content)
- test/privacy-patterns.test.mjs (high-confidence secret-like content)

Only continue if you reviewed these files and they contain safe fixtures/placeholders, not real secrets.
Block the commit if you are unsure.

Commit these flagged files?
```

## Non-UI unsafe-file refusal error

```text
CommitMe refused to create a commit because known secret files or high-confidence secret tokens, or unreadable changed files, would be staged: src/privacy/secret-patterns.ts, test/privacy-patterns.test.mjs. Remove them from the commit or commit them manually if intentional.
```
